senses.definition: having four leaves or leaflets
examples: A quadrifoliate leaf pattern appears on the border.
The plant displays quadrifoliate leaves in a symmetrical arrangement.

senses.definition: having four leaflets
examples: The quadrifoliolate compound leaf was clearly divided into four leaflets.
A quadrifoliolate arrangement enhances photosynthesis efficiency.

senses.definition: having four leaves or leaflets; four-leaved
examples: A quadrifolious plant was spotted in the garden.
The quadrifolious pattern repeats around the stem.

senses.definition: a symbol or figure with four leaves; a four-leafed clover or similar shape
examples: The shield bore a quadrifolium emblem.
A quadrifolium design decorates the tapestry.

senses.definition: having four forms, parts, or divisions
examples: The quadriform structure has four distinct sections.
A quadriform plan outlines four branches of study.

senses.definition: a person or thing with four foreheads or fronts; a four-faced object or figure
examples: The statue depicted a quadrifrons deity.
In heraldry, a quadrifrons design shows four frontal aspects.

senses.definition: having four fronts or frontal aspects
examples: The quadrifrontal sculpture features four projecting fronts.
A quadrifrontal arrangement presents four front-facing elements.

senses.definition: to divide into four branches or parts
examples: The river quadrifurcates near the valley.
A quadrifurcate stem splits into four main shoots.

senses.definition: divided into four branches
examples: A quadrifurcated riverbed was visible.
The quadrifurcated root system forms four main branches.

senses.definition: the act or result of dividing into four branches
examples: The quadrifurcation of the river created four channels.
We studied the quadrifurcation pattern in the plant's roots.

senses.definition: a chariot drawn by four horses abreast; a four-horse team in ancient Rome
examples: The quadriga pulled the ceremonial chariot.
The statue shows a quadriga with four horses.

senses.definition: having four gables or projecting sections on a roof
examples: A quadrigabled roof dominates the facade.
The quadrigabled mansion features four triangular gables.

senses.definition: plural of quadriga; four-horse chariots or teams
examples: Decorative panels feature several quadrigae.
The parade included multiple quadrigae in formation.

senses.definition: a person who has four spouses simultaneously; historically rare or problematic practice
examples: The term quadrigamist is rarely used in modern contexts.
Some historical texts mention quadrigamists in certain cultures.

senses.definition: plural of quadriga; four-horse chariots
examples: The coins depicted quadrigas in relief.
Ancient emblems often show quadrigas racing.

senses.definition: having four arches or openings; shaped like a four-gated or four-faced structure
examples: A quadrigate building features four gate-like entrances.
The quadrigate plan encloses a courtyard.

senses.definition: plural of quadrigatus; four-faced or four-ridged items; in some contexts, a religious or mythic four-faced figure
examples: Ancient inscriptions mention quadrigati in ritual contexts.
The artifact displays several quadrigati along the border.

senses.definition: a four-faced or four-edged object; specifically a type of ancient Roman coin or symbol with four faces
examples: The coin bore a quadrigatus motif.
Collectors seek quadrigatus specimens for their rarity.

senses.definition: relating to four twin structures or parts, especially in anatomy or geology
examples: The quadrigeminal plate is a neural structure in the brain.
Quadrigeminal surfaces describe a four-part arrangement in the specimen.

senses.definition: having four twin or paired parts; four-parted
examples: A quadrigeminate pattern repeats four times.
The quadrigeminate motif appears in the architectural frieze.

senses.definition: having four twin elements; fourfold in structure
examples: The quadrigeminous arrangement has four identical units.
A quadrigeminous cross shows four mirrored arms.

senses.definition: a four-twin structure or a fourfold element; used in botanical or anatomical naming
examples: The specimen was described as having a quadrigeminum arrangement.
In old texts, quadrigeminum denotes a four-twin configuration.

senses.definition: related to or containing four kinds or generations
examples: The quadrigenarious lineage spans four generations.
A quadrigenarious dataset includes four generational groups.

senses.definition: having four glands or gland-like structures
examples: The quadriglandular system is a hypothetical model in anatomy.
A quadriglandular organism would possess four glands.

senses.definition: a hybrid produced from four different parental lines or varieties
examples: The quadrihybrid shows traits from four distinct strains.
Researchers created a quadrihybrid to study trait distribution.
